My datagrid has items added but when I call this method itemsource is null why?

If the ItemsSource property of your DataGrid is null when you call the OrderDataGrid method, it means that the ItemsSource has not been set yet, or has been cleared from the DataGrid. Here are a few potential causes and solutions for this issue:

1. ItemsSource is not set yet: If your DataGrid is created dynamically in code-behind, it's possible that the ItemsSource property has not been set yet when you call the OrderDataGrid method. Make sure that you set the ItemsSource property with a collection that implements IList before calling the method.

2. ItemsSource has been cleared: If you've removed or cleared the ItemsSource property from the DataGrid at some point, you'll need to set it again before calling the method. It's also possible that the ItemsSource was set to null accidentally, so double-check that it's being set properly.

3. ItemsSource is being set asynchronously: If you're setting the ItemsSource property asynchronously (such as with an async method or a separate thread), it's possible that the OrderDataGrid method is being called before the ItemsSource is set. Consider using async/await or synchronization methods to ensure that the ItemsSource is set before calling the sorting method.

4. ItemsSource is being set on a different thread: If you're setting the ItemsSource property on a different thread than the one calling the OrderDataGrid method, you may need to use synchronization techniques like locks or Dispatcher.Invoke to ensure that the sorting method is only called after the ItemsSource is set.

In general, make sure that you're setting the ItemsSource property with a valid IList collection and that you're not accidentally nullifying it somewhere in your code. If you're still having trouble, try setting a breakpoint at the point where you set the ItemsSource property and step through the code to see when the OrderDataGrid method is being called and whether ItemsSource is null at that point.

ok the datagrid now select the correct index but it doesn't highlight it blue like when you select it manually, how can I do that?
what is the use for DataGridA.CurrentItem? Do I need that or with SelectedItem  and SelectedIndex  is enough?

The CurrentItem property of the DataGrid represents the current item that the user is interacting with in the UI, such as the currently edited or active row. It may or may not be the same as the selected item, depending on how your application is designed.

In most cases, you don't need to use the CurrentItem property unless you have a specific scenario where you want to get or set the current item separately from the selected item. For example, you might have a button that saves the current (edited) item without changing the selection in the DataGrid.

In your case, it sounds like you simply want to select and highlight a specific item in the DataGrid, which can be done with the SelectedItem and SelectedIndex properties. You don't need the CurrentItem property unless you have additional requirements for handling the currently active item in the UI.
it still doesn't highlight with blue the selected row, why is that?

If the selected row is not being highlighted with blue, it's possible that the DataGrid or its container is overriding the default selection styles. There are a few things that may be causing this issue:

1. Selection styles are overridden by a style or template: If you're using a custom style or template on your DataGrid or its container, it's possible that the selection styles are being overridden or are not being applied correctly. You may need to inspect the style or template and make sure that it includes the correct styles for the selected row.

2. Selection styles are set incorrectly: It's possible that the selection styles for the DataGrid are set incorrectly or are not being applied correctly in the XAML. You can check the XAML to make sure that the styles are being defined and applied correctly.

3. Selection styles are set by the system colors: The selection styles for the DataGrid may be set by the system colors, which can be customized in the Windows settings. If the colors are set to something other than blue, the selected row may not appear as expected. You can try resetting the system colors or checking the color settings to see if this is the issue.

4. Selection mode is incorrect: If the selection mode for the DataGrid is set to "None" instead of "Single", the rows may not highlight when clicked. Make sure that the selection mode is set correctly in the XAML or code-behind.

5. Items control container style is incorrect: If the DataGrid uses a Virtualized container with some involved Panels or VirtualizingStackPannel , it could be the item containers' virtualisation is not realised in real-time.

You can begin by checking the XAML to make sure that the styles are being applied correctly and inspecting the visual tree to see if any other styles or templates are interfering with the selection styles. It may also be helpful to create a new, simple DataGrid in a new project to see if the selection works as expected in a fresh environment.
it does highlight when I click it with the mouse but not with the code
how do I get the DataGridColumns from the datagrid? it is trhowing me an error

To get the DataGridColumns from a DataGrid, you can use the Columns property of the DataGrid. However, this property returns a collection of DataGridColumn objects, not strings. In order to set the CurrentCell property to the first column, you need to get the DataGridColumn object for the first column and pass it to the DataGridCellInfo constructor.

To get the DataGridColumn object for the first column of the DataGrid, you can use the following code:

```
var column = DataGridA.Columns[0];
DataGridA.CurrentCell = new DataGridCellInfo(DataGridA.SelectedItem, column);
```

This code gets the first DataGridColumn object from the DataGrid's Columns collection and passes it to the DataGridCellInfo constructor along with the selected item. This sets the CurrentCell property to the first column of the selected row, which should highlight the selection correctly.

It's important to note that the Columns property may be null or empty until the DataGrid is loaded and its template is applied, so you may need to check for this condition in your code. You can also use the AutoGeneratingColumn event of the DataGrid to dynamically generate columns and set the CurrentCell property based on the generated columns.
